ucs.title: [LLM] FIN10 PowerShell Empire stager + schtasks persistence chain
ucs.description: Hunts FIN10's signature post-exploitation chain: a PowerShell Empire/Meterpreter stager invoked from a non-interactive parent (wmic, services, or office) that within minutes registers a scheduled task pointing back at PowerShell or rundll32. FireEye's 2017 FIN10 report specifically called out scheduled-task persistence (often masquerading as 'Adobe' / 'Java' updaters) chained to PowerShell Empire HTTP listeners on the same host.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as ps_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as ps_parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe") (Processes.process="*-NoP*" OR Processes.process="*-NonI*" OR Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*System.Net.WebClient*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as ps_dest user as ps_user | join type=inner ps_dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as task_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("schtasks.exe","at.exe") Processes.process="*/create*" (Processes.process="*powershell*" OR Processes.process="*rundll32*" OR Processes.process="*regsvr32*" OR Processes.process="*Adobe*" OR Processes.process="*Java*" OR Processes.process="*Google*Update*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as ps_dest] | where abs(_time - _time) < 600 | table firstTime ps_dest ps_user ps_parent ps_cmd task_cmd | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// FIN10 — PowerShell Empire stager followed by schtasks persistence on same host within 10 min
let WindowMin = 10m;
let PSStagers =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-NoP","-NonI","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","FromBase64String","IEX(","Invoke-Expression","DownloadString","System.Net.WebClient","Net.WebRequest","Empire")
    | project StagerTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              StagerParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, StagerCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("schtasks.exe","at.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","-create"," /sc "," /tr ")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("powershell","rundll32","regsvr32","mshta","wscript","cscript","cmd.exe",@"\Users\Public\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/tn\s+\"?(adobe|java|google|microsoft|windows)?\s*(update|updater|task|maint|defrag|run)"
| project TaskTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
          TaskCmd=ProcessCommandLine, TaskParent=InitiatingProcessFileName
| join kind=inner PSStagers on DeviceId
| where TaskTime between (StagerTime .. StagerTime + WindowMin)
| project StagerTime, TaskTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TaskTime, StagerTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, StagerParent, StagerCmd, TaskParent, TaskCmd
| order by StagerTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN10 — PS Empire stager + schtasks persistence (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let WindowMin = 10m;
let PSStagers =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "powershell_ise.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "pwsh.exe"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("-NoP","-NonI","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","FromBase64String","IEX(","Invoke-Expression","DownloadString","System.Net.WebClient","Empire")
    | project StagerTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              StagerParent=ParentProcessName, StagerCmd=CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "schtasks.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "at.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","-create"," /sc "," /tr ")
| where CommandLine has_any ("powershell","rundll32","regsvr32","mshta","wscript","cscript","cmd.exe",@"\Users\Public\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/tn\s+\"?(adobe|java|google|microsoft|windows)?\s*(update|updater|task|maint|defrag|run)"
| project TaskTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          TaskCmd=CommandLine, TaskParent=ParentProcessName
| join kind=inner PSStagers on Computer
| where TaskTime between (StagerTime .. StagerTime + WindowMin)
| project StagerTime, TaskTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TaskTime, StagerTime),
          Computer, Account, StagerParent, StagerCmd, TaskParent, TaskCmd
| order by StagerTime desc
ucs.rationale: FIN10's installation tradecraft is documented (FireEye, June 2017) as PowerShell Empire stagers paired with schtasks-based persistence using lure names like 'Adobe Update' or 'Java Update' pointing at PowerShell/rundll32 payloads in C:\Users\Public or %AppData%. Most APTs use one or the other; the same-host, sub-10-minute pairing of PS Empire-style cmdline plus schtasks /create with a PS/rundll32

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN10 RDP lateral movement to data-staging share with valid stolen accounts
ucs.description: Hunts FIN10's post-foothold lateral movement: an interactive RDP logon using a non-machine account from an internal hop-host followed within an hour by archive/staging activity (.7z/.rar/.zip creation) on the destination — the FireEye-documented FIN10 path from initial compromise to bulk data theft used to extort gaming/mining victims. Catches the actor's preference for valid accounts + RDP rather than C2-driven lateral movement.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as logonTime from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.authentication_method="Kerberos" OR Authentication.authentication_method="NTLM" Authentication.signature_id IN (4624) Authentication.logon_type=10 NOT Authentication.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","ANONYMOUS LOGON") by Authentication.dest Authentication.user Authentication.src _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | rename dest as host user as actor | join type=inner host actor [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Filesystem.file_name) as staged_files values(Filesystem.file_path) as staged_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_name="*.7z" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.rar" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.zip" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.cab") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host user as actor] | where abs(_time - logonTime) < 3600 AND _time >= logonTime | table logonTime _time host actor src staged_files staged_paths count | `security_content_ctime(logonTime)`
ucs.kql:
// FIN10 — RDP logon (LogonType 10) followed by archive staging on same host within 60m
let StageWindow = 60m;
let RdpLogons =
    DeviceLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | where LogonType == "RemoteInteractive"
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service","anonymous logon")
    | where RemoteIPType in ("Private","Loopback")    // internal hop, not external RDP gateway
    | project LogonTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SourceHost=RemoteDeviceName, SourceIP=RemoteIP, LogonId;
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FileName endswith ".7z" or FileName endswith ".rar" or FileName endswith ".zip" or FileName endswith ".cab" or FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(00[0-9]|r0[0-9])$"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Windows\Tasks\", @"\PerfLogs\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe","makecab.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
| project StageTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
          ArchiveFile=FileName, ArchivePath=FolderPath,
          StageActor=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          StageCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner RdpLogons on DeviceId
| where StageTime between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + StageWindow)
| where StageActor =~ AccountName
| project LogonTime, StageTime,
          MinutesAfterLogon = datetime_diff('minute', StageTime, LogonTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, SourceHost, SourceIP,
          ArchiveFile, ArchivePath, StageCmd
| order by LogonTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN10 — RDP logon (4624 LogonType 10) + archive staging on same host within 60m
let StageWindow = 60m;
let RdpLogons =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4624
    | where LogonType == 10
    | where TargetUserName !endswith "$"
    | where TargetUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","ANONYMOUS LOGON","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")
    | where ipv4_is_private(IpAddress)
    | project LogonTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account=TargetUserName,
              SourceIP=IpAddress, SourceHost=WorkstationName, TargetLogonId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where (CommandLine has_any ("7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe","makecab.exe")
         or NewProcessName endswith "7z.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "rar.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "winrar.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any (" a "," -m"," -hp"," -p","-r ")
| where CommandLine has_any (".7z",".rar",".zip",".cab",@"\Users\Public\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Temp\",@"\PerfLogs\")
| project StageTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account=SubjectUserName,
          StageCmd=CommandLine, StageProc=NewProcessName
| join kind=inner RdpLogons on Computer
| where StageTime between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + StageWindow)
| where Account =~ tostring(split(RdpLogons.Account,"@")[0])
| project LogonTime, StageTime,
          MinutesAfterLogon = datetime_diff('minute', StageTime, LogonTime),
          Computer, Account, SourceHost, SourceIP,
          StageProc, StageCmd
| order by LogonTime desc
ucs.rationale: FIN10's intrusion outcome — per the FireEye 2017 cluster report — is bulk data theft used to extort North American mining and casino victims. Their lateral movement pattern is RDP with stolen valid accounts (T1078 + T1021.001), not C2-driven SMB pivots, and they stage exfil archives in writable directories like C:\Users\Public or C:\PerfLogs before exfiltration. The chained pattern (interactive RD
